children.heading: Appropriate State regulatory authority
children.label: 1 Appropriate State regulatory authority
children.text: The term “appropriate State regulatory authority” means the State agency or other authority responsible for the licensure or other regulation of the practice of accounting in the State or States having jurisdiction over a registered public accounting firm or associated person thereof, with respect to the matter in question.

children.heading: Audit
children.label: 2 Audit
children.text: The term “audit” means an examination of the financial statements of any issuer by an independent public accounting firm in accordance with the rules of the Board or the Commission (or, for the period preceding the adoption of applicable rules of the Board under , in accordance with then-applicable generally accepted auditing and related standards for such purposes), for the purpose of expressing an opinion on such statements.section 7213 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a committee (or equivalent body) established by and amongst the board of directors of an issuer for the purpose of overseeing the accounting and financial reporting processes of the issuer and audits of the financial statements of the issuer; and

children.label: B
children.text: if no such committee exists with respect to an issuer, the entire board of directors of the issuer.

children.label: A
children.text: prepared following an audit performed for purposes of compliance by an issuer with the requirements of the securities laws; and

children.label: i
children.text: sets forth the opinion of that firm regarding a financial statement, report, or other document; or

children.label: ii
children.text: asserts that no such opinion can be expressed.

children.heading: Board
children.label: 5 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board established under .section 7211 of this title

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 6 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Securities and Exchange Commission.

children.heading: Issuer
children.label: 7 Issuer
children.text: The term “issuer” means an issuer (as defined in ), the securities of which are registered under section 78 of this title, or that is required to file reports under section 78(d) of this title, or that files or has filed a registration statement that has not yet become effective under the Securities Act of 1933 ( et seq.), and that it has not withdrawn.section 78c of this title15 U.S.C. 77alo

children.heading: Non-audit services
children.label: 8 Non-audit services
children.text: The term “non-audit services” means any professional services provided to an issuer by a registered public accounting firm, other than those provided to an issuer in connection with an audit or a review of the financial statements of an issuer.

children.heading: Person associated with a public accounting firm
children.label: 9 Person associated with a public accounting firm
i
children.text: shares in the profits of, or receives compensation in any other form from, that firm; or

children.label: ii
children.text: participates as agent or otherwise on behalf of such accounting firm in any activity of that firm.

children.heading: Exemption authority
children.label: B Exemption authority
children.text: The Board may, by rule, exempt persons engaged only in ministerial tasks from the definition in subparagraph (A), to the extent that the Board determines that any such exemption is consistent with the purposes of this Act, the public interest, or the protection of investors.

children.label: i
children.text: the authority to conduct an investigation of such person under shall apply only with respect to any act or practice, or omission to act, by the person while such person was associated or seeking to become associated with a registered public accounting firm; andsection 7215(b) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: conduct occurring while such person was associated or seeking to become associated with a registered public accounting firm; or

children.label: II
children.text: non-cooperation, as described in , with respect to a demand in a Board investigation for testimony, documents, or other information relating to a period when such person was associated or seeking to become associated with a registered public accounting firm.section 7215(b)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: established by the standard setting body described in section 19(b) of the Securities Act of 1933 [], or prescribed by the Commission under section 19(a) of that Act [] or ; and15 U.S.C. 77s(b)15 U.S.C. 77s(a)section 78m(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: relevant to audit reports for particular issuers, or dealt with in the quality control system of a particular registered public accounting firm; and

children.label: i
children.text: relate to the preparation or issuance of audit reports for issuers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: are established or adopted by the Board under , or are promulgated as rules of the Commission.section 7213(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a proprietorship, partnership, incorporated association, corporation, limited liability company, limited liability partnership, or other legal entity that is engaged in the practice of public accounting or preparing or issuing audit reports; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent so designated by the rules of the Board, any associated person of any entity described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Registered public accounting firm
children.label: 12 Registered public accounting firm
children.text: The term “registered public accounting firm” means a public accounting firm registered with the Board in accordance with this Act.

children.heading: Rules of the Board
children.label: 13 Rules of the Board
children.text: The term “rules of the Board” means the bylaws and rules of the Board (as submitted to, and approved, modified, or amended by the Commission, in accordance with ), and those stated policies, practices, and interpretations of the Board that the Commission, by rule, may deem to be rules of the Board, as necessary or appropriate in the public interest or for the protection of investors.section 7217 of this title

children.heading: Security
children.label: 14 Security
children.text: The term “security” has the same meaning as in .section 78c(a) of this title

children.heading: Securities laws
children.label: 15 Securities laws
children.text: The term “securities laws” means the provisions of law referred to in and includes the rules, regulations, and orders issued by the Commission thereunder.section 78c(a)(47) of this title

children.heading: State
children.label: 16 State
children.text: The term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, or any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: Foreign auditor oversight authority
children.label: 17 Foreign auditor oversight authority
children.text: The term “foreign auditor oversight authority” means any governmental body or other entity empowered by a foreign government to conduct inspections of public accounting firms or otherwise to administer or enforce laws related to the regulation of public accounting firms.
